operating_state: Rental revenues increased to $6.8 billion from $4.9 billion driven by Duke Transaction acquisition in October 2022 and additional U.S. portfolio acquisition in June 2023. Market rent growth continued globally with rent change on rollover reaching 76.4% on net effective basis while occupancy remained at 97.6%. Same store property NOI grew 7.8% reflecting cumulative market rent growth and lease mark-to-market opportunities. Core FFO increased to $5.3 billion from $4.2 billion.
cost_structure: Rental expenses increased proportionally to portfolio expansion from acquisitions. G&A expenses rose due to inflationary increases, Prologis Essentials expansion, and higher performance-based compensation. Depreciation and amortization expenses increased from Duke Transaction assets and development completions. Current income tax expense on dispositions doubled while deferred income tax expense remained stable.
material_events: Completed Duke Transaction integration and acquired additional U.S. portfolio in June 2023. Generated $2.1 billion in net proceeds from property contributions to co-investment ventures and third-party sales, realizing net gains. Formed PJLF unconsolidated venture in Japan with 16.3% ownership and earned promotes from USLF third-party investors. Issued $5.4 billion in senior notes across multiple currencies with 4.7% weighted average interest rate. Leadership transition includes Gary Anderson moving to senior adviser with Carter Andrus becoming COO and Joseph Ghazal becoming CIO effective January 2024.
macro_exposures: Interest expense increased significantly to $641 million from $309 million due to higher rates on new debt issuances with weighted average effective rate rising to 2.8% from 1.8%. Foreign currency exposure from global operations partially hedged through functional currency borrowings generated unrealized losses versus prior year gains. Weather-related insurance costs increased from greater frequency of weather events.
forward_outlook: Development portfolio expected completion before July 2026 with 37.9% pre-leasing requiring additional investment. Management expects continued organic revenue growth from cumulative market rent increases and high lease mark-to-market spread. Strategic capital segment positioned for ongoing fee generation from co-investment venture portfolio with no significant promote opportunities anticipated for 2024.
uncertainty_profile: Total debt portfolio of $29.0 billion with 9-year weighted average maturity provides refinancing flexibility though exposed to interest rate environment changes. Co-investment ventures carry non-recourse debt requiring refinancing management across multiple markets. Development completion subject to market absorption rates and construction delivery schedules. REIT distribution requirements constrain capital allocation flexibility while maintaining tax-qualified status.
